title: Leptidium filicaule
intro:

Here's a paragraph about garden cress:

Garden cress (Leptidium sativum) is a cool-season annual herb in the mustard family, characterized by its peppery flavor and fragrance. The health benefits of garden cress include its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of garden cress include isothiocyanates, such as allyl isothiocyanate, which have been shown to have anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Garden cress is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in salads and as a garnish to add flavor and nutrition.

This family includes a wide range of species, from weeds and wildflowers to cultivated crops. The Brassicaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Leptidium filicaule being a part of the tribe Lepidieae.
